name: GC1 mock guard misses multiline jest.mock calls
description: The GC1 guard previously used only a single-line regex (MOCK_LINE) and missed jest.mock( calls where the specifier was on a subsequent line. This left multiline internal mocks undetected, bypassing the ratchet silently.
acceptanceCriteria: Root cause: scripts/check-gc1-pattern-a.ts — prior to commit 15f388414 the guard used only MOCK_LINE (single-line regex) and had no MOCK_OPEN / SPECIFIER_LINE path. Multiline jest.mock( calls with the specifier on a separate line were invisible to the scanner. Fix shipped in PR #1117 [F-156] (commit 15f388414): added MOCK_OPEN regex at :38 and SPECIFIER_LINE at :40, plus a 15-line look-ahead loop at :142-169 that accumulates the span and detects the specifier. Tests added in scripts/check-gc1-pattern-a.test.ts lines 119-131 ('detects a multiline internal mock') and lines 147-162 ('with comment+blank lines before specifier') — these are the regression tests. Verification: run 'pnpm exec jest --config scripts/jest.config.cjs scripts/check-gc1-pattern-a.test.ts --no-coverage' — all multiline cases must pass. Single behavioral variant: the multiline detection is now covered. NOTE: this fix has already shipped. This WI should be verified closed — run the test suite and transition to Done if all cases pass.
bodyText:
Brief
The detector only applies MOCK_LINE to each added diff line independently, so a new internal mock written as a multiline call such as jest.mock(\n  './services/foo',\n  () => ({ ... })\n) has no single added line matching the regex and is not checked for Pattern A or gc1-allow. This weakens the CI/pre-commit ratchet and can allow broad internal mocks to land despite the stated guard. Prettier will collapse simple cases, but long or commented call expressions can remain multiline.
Recommended remediation
Parse staged/HEAD test files with the TypeScript AST and inspect jest.mock/jest.doMock call expressions whose start line was added, or assemble multiline added hunks before matching.
DeepSec finding
Finding ID: DS-234
Scan run: 20260516050543-3902c61be7bd5834 (2026-05-16, deepsec process / gpt-5.5)
File: scripts/check-gc1-pattern-a.ts — lines 35, 89, 103, 105
Vulnerability class: other-guard-bypass
Severity: BUG · Confidence: medium
Notes
Captured from the DeepSec scan artefact .deepsec/data/eduagent-build/reports/report.json. Part of bundle WI-76 (WP-ACL). Acceptance Criteria and Execution Path are set during triage/refinement.
